era: 1959-present. Garage Rock. E=7 -- 'Louie Louie.' Portland origin, Northwest circuit. Wand Records.

louie_louie.recording_notes: Microphone mounted too high; Jack Ely (wearing braces) sang at upward angle creating the distinctive slurred vocal; Don Gallucci on keyboards was reportedly 14-15 years old

fbi_investigation.conclusion: Lyrics of the recording could not be interpreted; not possible to determine if obscene
fbi_investigation.result: No charges filed; song became more famous

louie_louie.three_chord_structure: A - D - Em (the most democratic rock song ever written; any band can play it)

richard_berry.also_wrote: Have Love Will Travel (covered by The Sonics, degree 241)
richard_berry.rights: Sold rights for small sum before song's commercial peak; received little from national success
richard_berry.note: Richard Berry connects The Sonics (241) and The Kingsmen (244) through authorship of both bands' most notable covers.

theory_nodes.concept: "Louie Louie" as the most famous garage rock song ever recorded. #2 Billboard December 1963. Three chords. $50. Jack Ely's braces and a mispositioned microphone created the unintelligible vocal that the FBI investigated for two years. The limitations were the art.
theory_nodes.description: Louie Louie as the defining garage rock cultural monument.

theory_nodes.concept: FBI investigation 1964-65: two years, ~$100,000, conclusion 'lyrics could not be interpreted.' Hoover-era FBI treating rock and roll as potential national security threat. Investigation made the song more famous, not less. The federal government was the best marketing campaign the Kingsmen ever had.

Richard Berry connection: 'Have Love Will Travel' (Sonics, 241) AND 'Louie Louie' (Kingsmen, 244) both written by Richard Berry. One songwriter links the proto-punk maximum and the most famous garage rock recording. Berry sold rights and received little from either song's commercial success.
theory_nodes.description: Richard Berry as connecting thread between degrees 241 and 244.

cross_references.relationship: Richard Berry connection: 'Have Love Will Travel' (Sonics) + 'Louie Louie' (Kingsmen). Same songwriter, two iconic Northwest Sound recordings.
Wailers recorded 'Louie Louie' before the Kingsmen. The song was a PNW standard before 1963.
Recorded their own 'Louie Louie' at the same Portland studio, possibly the same day. Kingsmen's version became the definitive recording.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 vs E=4. FIRST ACIPENSERIDAE. 200+ million year lineage. Largest freshwater fish in North America. The most famous garage rock song against North America's largest freshwater fish.

milestones: 'LOUIE LOUIE' -- MOST FAMOUS GARAGE ROCK SONG EVER RECORDED
